arts.ttl: Serie A: Monza vs Roma – probable line-ups and where to watch on TV
arts.sum: Under-pressure Roma coach Ivan Juric meets Alessandro Nesta’s Monza, the only Serie A team without...

Roma have won three of their last four games (D1) against Monza in Serie A; the Giallorossi could become the first team that the Brianza side have faced five times in the tournament without ever winning. Meanwhile, the Giallorossi have won both of their league matches – against Udinese and Venezia – since the appointment of Ivan Juric and are aiming for a third consecutive victory to go into their big clash against Inter after the international break with real momentum.

arts.ttl: Roma Set to Take on Monza Without Attacking Mainstone
arts.sum: Roma will seek their third consecutive Serie A triumph under new manager Ivan Juric without standout performer Paulo Dybala. The Giallorossi took to X to announce their matchday squad for Sunday's fixture against Monza, with the Argentine's name left out. arts.ttl: Roma face setback as Dybala and Hummels ruled out for Monza match
arts.sum: Roma have been dealt a blow ahead of their Serie A match against Monza, with key players Paulo Dybala and Mats Hummels both absent from the squad list. Dybala, who missed Roma’s previous league fixture against Venezia, was expected to make his return but has been sidelined once again. The Argentine forward experienced discomfort during training, prompting the club to take a cautious approach with the international break looming. Roma’s medical staff decided not to risk aggravating Dybala’s condition, opting to give him more time to recover. arts.ttl: Juric: “We’ve had little time to prepare for Monza. We need to raise our level”
arts.sum: Roma manager Ivan Juric addressed the media ahead of his team’s Serie A match against Monza, emphasizing the need for improvement and adaptation in a congested fixture list. Juric began by highlighting the team’s potential for growth: “In this period, we have a large margin for improvement, both in terms of ideas and as a team. To compete well on all fronts, we need to use as many players as possible.” Reflecting on the recent Europa League defeat to Elfsborg, Juric acknowledged areas for improvement: “I think we had several clear chances. arts.ttl: Nesta jokes about referee’s allegiance ahead of Monza-Roma clash
arts.sum: Monza manager Alessandro Nesta displayed his sense of humor during a press conference ahead of his team’s match against Roma. When asked about the appointment of Federico La Penna, a referee from Rome, to officiate the game, Nesta quipped, “Maybe he’s a Lazio fan,” before adding more seriously, “Jokes aside, I don’t think about these dynamics.” The issue of referee territoriality has been a topic of discussion in Italian football, but Nesta’s lighthearted response suggests he’s not concerned about potential bias. Discussing the upcoming match, Nesta praised Roma’s new manager Ivan Juric: “We’ve studied them.
